name: InterpretML (ML Explainability Toolkit)

performance.notes: EBM training is slow (Rust backend); SHAP/LIME explanations depend on model size

agent_readiness.notes: Unified ML explainability with glass-box and black-box methods. from interpret.glassbox import ExplainableBoostingClassifier. ebm = ExplainableBoostingClassifier(). ebm.fit(X_train, y_train). ebm.explain_global() → shape functions per feature. ebm.explain_local(X_test[:5]) → per-instance. from interpret.blackbox import ShapKernel, LimeTabular. shap = ShapKernel(predict_fn, X_train). exp = shap.explain_local(X_test). from interpret import show. show(ebm.explain_global()). show(exp). EBM is linear in prediction time, as accurate as GBM. Morris sensitivity. PDP. Dashboard: show([ebm_global, exp], 8080).

gotchas: EBM training slower than XGBoost/LightGBM for same accuracy
show() dashboard requires Jupyter or local web server
BlackBox explainers (SHAP, LIME) are wrappers — not as optimized as standalone shap library
EBM doesn't support all sklearn estimators in pipeline directly
best_when: Explainable Boosting Machine (EBM) for interpretable-by-design ML model
Regulatory-compliant ML where interpretability is mandatory (credit, healthcare)
Unified dashboard comparing multiple explanation methods
Glass-box alternative to black-box GBMs with similar accuracy
avoid_when: Deep learning explanations (use captum or tf-explain)
When standalone shap library is sufficient
Production API (no REST interface — Jupyter/local only)
summary: InterpretML provides glass-box (EBM) and black-box (SHAP, LIME) ML explanations. ExplainableBoostingClassifier: interpretable-by-design GBM. explain_global() → feature shape functions. explain_local() → per-instance. ShapKernel, LimeTabular wrappers. show() interactive dashboard. MIT by Microsoft Research. Best for regulatory-compliant ML and EBM as interpretable GBM alternative. Dashboard requires Jupyter. EBM trains slower than XGBoost.
